title: RESILIENCE AND RECOVERY PROJECT - 211info
text: PATHFINDER NETWORK JACKSON COUNTY RESILIENCE & RECOVERY PROJECT RESILIENCE AND RECOVERY PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Peer support program for justice system-impacted individuals. Peer Support Specialists with lived experience engage participants in a goal-oriented collaborative relationship and provide personalized support through holistic and responsive individual and group support services. Works to reinforce successes and build self efficacy, hope, and resilience. 48 Hawthorne Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with current or prior justice system involvement Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 231-0765 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 231-0765 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, email, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Jackson County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Offender/Ex-Offender Support Groups Peer Mental Health Support Services * Offender/Ex-Offender Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
